summary: A young Roman knight (eques Romanus) and close connection of Cicero, distinguished in every respect and attentive to him. Cicero had a long friendship with his father dating from his own quaestorship in Sicily, and commends Bruttius's household, estate, and agents to the proconsul M'. Acilius Glabrio in 46 BC (Fam. 13.38).

The last surviving member of the Titurnian family (familia Titurnia), with whom Cicero had a long-standing tie of connection. Cicero commends him warmly to the proconsul M'. Acilius Glabrio in Sicily, asking that he find sufficient protection through Cicero's recommendation (Fam. 13.39, 46 BC).

A town in southern Illyria (Illyricum), on the route between Apollonia and Dyrrachium. Its citizens (the Bulliones) undertook to give satisfaction to L. Lucceius at Pompey's arbitration, the business Cicero presses on the proconsul Culleolus (Fam. 13.42).
